metadata.notes: agy backgrounds commands (WaitMsBeforeAsync) but emits no backgroundTaskId/PID; transcript shows one DONE result, no held-working arc — incapable, frozen

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready
route: frozen

## Reasoning

**Agent (yes).** Antigravity can launch a long-running command in the background. Its `run_command` tool has a required `WaitMsBeforeAsync` arg whose schema reads: "the number of milliseconds to wait after starting the command before sending it to the BACKGROUND ... If you're starting an interactive or long-running command, set it to a large enough value ...". A real sample transcript shows `WaitMsBeforeAsync:"5000"` on every `run_command`. There is also a separate command-status tool ("Number of seconds to wait for command completion before getting the status ... Set to 0 to get the status immediately"). So the agent genuinely supports a backgrounded process plus a poll-for-status, the moral equivalent of run_in_background + BashOutput.

**Daemon (incapable).** The scenario's user-observable arc is `BackgroundProcessCount > 0` while the detached process is alive and the session HELD `working` past the turn's own end_turn, settling to `ready` only once the process exits. For Antigravity the daemon cannot reconstruct this:

- No `backgroundTaskId`, PID, or output-file handle is emitted to the transcript. claudecode's #450 mechanism keys entirely on `toolUseResult.backgroundTaskId` + an `lsof` probe of `tasks/<id>.output`; the Antigravity `RUN_COMMAND` step carries no such id. In every real sample, RUN_COMMAND statuses are uniformly `DONE` — the async command reconciles to a SINGLE terminal result line, with no spawn event and no separate completion event.
- The adapter parser has NO BackgroundSpawn path. `parseToolResult` simply closes the open tool (emitting `function_call_output`) and reads success/failure from the result text; the antigravity package has ZERO references to `BackgroundProcess`, `BackgroundSpawn`, or `backgroundTaskId` (grep over `core/adapters/inbound/agents/antigravity/`). There is no `HasLiveBackgroundProcess` gate, so `IsAgentDone` is not held — the terminal `PLANNER_RESPONSE` with no tool_calls settles the session to `ready` at turn end.
- Because the background command's life is mediated by `WaitMsBeforeAsync` inside the agent and surfaces as one DONE result, the daemon sees a normal working→ready turn, never `BackgroundProcessCount > 0` and never a held-working dwell.

No background-process liveness signal exists in the transcript Source, so `daemon_capability` is `incapable` (no trace), not `bug`.

**Driver (ready).** Daemon-frozen, so the driver axis parks at `ready` per the #476 convention.

## What would flip the verdict

Antigravity would have to emit a structured background-task id / output handle into the transcript (so the daemon could probe liveness) AND the adapter grow a BackgroundSpawn parse path + a liveness gate on IsAgentDone, mirroring claudecode's #450 design.
caveats: agent_supports=yes is grounded in the run_command WaitMsBeforeAsync schema + the separate command-status tool, confirmed by a real transcript sample (WaitMsBeforeAsync:5000); it is not a live recording of a long-running background process.

The async command's completion is mediated inside the agent (WaitMsBeforeAsync) and surfaces as a single DONE result line — there is no spawn/exit pair the daemon could bracket a held-working window around.

sources.note: parseToolResult closes the open tool as function_call_output; no BackgroundSpawn / backgroundTaskId path; terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE settles ready at turn end
Every run_command carries WaitMsBeforeAsync:5000; all RUN_COMMAND statuses are DONE (single terminal result, no spawn/exit pair, no PID/handle)
run_command WaitMsBeforeAsync schema ('sending it to the background'); separate command-status tool ('wait for command completion before getting the status')
Contrast: claudecode's BackgroundProcessCount keys on toolUseResult.backgroundTaskId + lsof probe — none of which antigravity emits or the adapter parses
